summary: This notebook implements a hybrid inference pipeline for RNA 3D structure prediction that combines homology modeling, diffusion-based inference, and geometric post-processing. It first attempts Template-Based Modeling (TBM) by aligning query sequences against a training pool, adapting known coordinates, and applying structural constraints and diversity transforms. For targets lacking sufficient templates or exceeding length limits, it routes them to a pre-trained Protenix model, handling long sequences via overlapping chunking and Kabsch-aligned stitching. Finally, it fills remaining prediction slots with a de-novo idealized A-form helix fallback. Readers learn how to systematically combine multiple prediction sources and enforce physical plausibility to generate diverse, submission-ready RNA conformations.
competition_flow: Loads test sequences and training structures, generates up to 10 predictions per target via TBM and Protenix inference with chunk stitching, fills gaps with a de-novo helix fallback, and saves the final coordinates to a CSV submission file.
data_reading: pd.read_csv for test_sequences.csv, train_sequences.csv, validation_sequences.csv, and their corresponding labels CSVs
Parsing stoichiometry and chain segmentation strings from CSV columns into multi-chain segment maps
data_processing: Global sequence alignment via Biopython PairwiseAligner for template matching
Coordinate adaptation to query length with NaN interpolation for gaps
Overlapping chunk splitting for sequences exceeding MAX_SEQ_LEN
Kabsch alignment and linear weight blending for stitching chunk coordinates
Adaptive RNA constraints (5.95Å backbone bonds, 10.2Å i-i+2 distances, Laplacian smoothing, self-avoidance)
Diversity transforms (hinge rotation, chain jitter, spline wiggle)
features_engineering: Domain-derived geometric constraints (backbone bond lengths, i-i+2 distances, Laplacian smoothing, self-avoidance)
Stoichiometry parsing for multi-chain complex handling
Idealized A-form helix coordinates for de-novo fallback
ensembling: Combines TBM homology predictions, Protenix diffusion outputs, and de-novo fallback coordinates to reach a fixed sample count per target, with no explicit post-processing beyond coordinate clipping.
insights: TBM effectively captures local structure when high-similarity templates are available, reducing reliance on heavy inference.
Overlapping chunking with Kabsch alignment and linear blending prevents geometric artifacts in long sequences.
Adaptive constraints and diversity transforms improve structural realism and sample diversity without requiring retraining.
A simple geometric fallback ensures complete coverage and submission validity for targets with no templates or failed inference.
critical_findings: Collapsed coordinates are explicitly detected during Protenix inference and filtered to prevent invalid submissions.
Stoichiometry parsing is required to correctly handle multi-chain RNA complexes that span multiple sequence segments.
Chunk stitching relies on a minimum overlap length (≥3 residues) to compute reliable Kabsch alignments; shorter overlaps are skipped.
